channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How do you do ??
item.description: From another programming language and recently picking in GO. I'm trying to understand how do you do things let suppose i have many services and storage,handler one things i often hear is that...

item.title: My verdict layer had two readers. Only one of them had eyes.
item.description: An internal release agent finished a deploy a little after 2 a.m. and then had nothing it could read. The dashboards were green. But green is something you see, not something you fetch. The...

item.title: The 5+5 Safest and Most Dangerous Software Roles in the Age of AI
item.description: Last month, we were developing a new AI-powered planning module for operator screens in a manufacturing ERP. Seeing how quickly AI generated code and easily integrated complex algorithms, I paused...

item.title: Machine learning in production: the model is the easy part
item.description: A model that scores 95% on your test set feels like the finish line. Then you ship it, and you find out it was the starting line. The model was maybe 10% of the work; everything that makes it...

item.title: Naming many view transitions: attr() or match-element?
item.description: You have a CSS grid of cards. You want a view transition that animates every card from its old position to its new one, individually. The rule is non-negotiable: each snapshot needs a unique...

item.title: Lucide 1.0 launches with over 1,600 icons and brand icons removed for leaner builds
item.description: Lucide 1.0 is here, and it changes the way developers ship icons. A 32% bundle size reduction for React, no more legal gray area with brand marks, over 1,600 consistently styled SVGs — all in a...

item.title: How I Stopped Overpaying For AI Models (And You Can Too)
item.description: Check this out: how I Stopped Overpaying For AI Models (And You Can Too) honestly, I gotta say... I'm pretty bad at math when it comes to my own money. I was burning through cash on AI...

item.title: Stop Guessing What’s Public: Automating Attack Surface Discovery
item.description: The "Forgotten Server" Problem Every developer has been there. You spin up a temporary staging instance to test a deployment script, or you launch a quick Redis container to debug a caching issue....

item.title: World Cup 2026: How the 48-Team Format Is Mathematically Tilting the Odds Toward Underdogs
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup in North America is reshaping one of sports' most fundamental probabilities: the likelihood of a "Cinderella story" advancing from the group stage. For the first time in...

item.title: RAG in production: the failure modes nobody warns you about
item.description: Retrieval-augmented generation looks trivial in a tutorial: embed some documents, drop them in a vector database, stuff the top matches into a prompt, done. Then you point it at real company data...

item.title: The best robot vacuum deals available during Prime Day
item.description: If you’ve been wanting to buy a robot vacuum but have been put off by how much it can cost to get a good one, now is not a bad time to start looking. Prime Day has kicked off, though more than...

item.title: This year’s Prime Day deals on Apple products are the best I’ve seen
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day sale is here, and whether you’re looking for a new pair of wireless earbuds or a smartwatch, there’s a good chance you’ll find a discount. The Apple Watch Series 11 has already...

item.title: India’s MoEngage bets that the future of marketing is millions of AI agents
item.description: The all-cash deal gives MoEngage access to technology that assigns AI agents to individual customers.

item.title: The Nex Playground is down to its pre-RAMageddon price during Prime Day
item.description: The Nex Playground is the family-centric, Kinect-like game console that made one Verge editor’s kids laugh, cry, and ask for more playtime, even when they were sick. The motion-based game play...

item.title: White House drastically shortens deadline for dropping quantum-vulnerable crypto
item.description: Order warns of national security risks if post-quantum cryptography isn't adopted in time.

item.title: Google Home will soon get better at recognizing you
item.description: A new update for Google Home could make it less likely your smart home cameras mistake you for someone else, just because you're facing away from the camera. Starting June 23rd, Google's expanding...

item.title: Hollywood is bending the knee to OpenAI
item.description: Netflix, A24, Focus Features, and Warner Bros.' Clockwork have all reportedly decided to pass on picking up Artificial - director Luca Guadagnino's new biographical drama about OpenAI cofounder /...

item.title: Heads in the game
item.description: The Argentina v. France final of the 2022 Men’s World Cup in Qatar was shaping up to be one of the most epic games in soccer history. With just 12 minutes remaining in the extra time added to the...

item.title: Super Mario is mathier than you think
item.description: Here’s a problem you probably didn’t solve in school: You’re an ambitious young plumber from Brooklyn in a world inhabited by violent human-size mushrooms called Goombas. The love of your life has...

item.title: A man of many words
item.description: Brian Sietsema has a favorite word. It’s somewhat surprising that he can choose just one. He’s the person spellers rely on to confirm pronunciations and answer questions about the roots of the...

item.title: Sharing a love for calculus
item.description: The national conversation about the value of education is currently dominated by speculation about the risks and positive potential of AI. Whatever your own perspective on that debate, I hope...

item.title: Stand Up for Research, Innovation, and Education
item.description: Right now, MIT alumni and friends are voicing their support for: America’s scientific and technological leadership Merit-based admissions and affordable education Advances that increase US health,...

item.title: Ultrasound imaging turns a robot hand into a skillful mimic
item.description: Our hands are the nimblest parts of our bodies, coordinating 34 muscles, 27 joints, and over 100 tendons and ligaments to perform countless nuanced movements and gestures. So far, robots have been...

item.title: Reinventing the zipper
item.description: With an adaptable fastener designed at CSAIL, pitching a tent or adjusting the cast for a broken bone could be almost as easy as zipping your coat. The researchers, led by associate professor...

item.title: Engineered “mini livers” could be injected as an alternative to transplantation
item.description: A technology developed by Professor Sangeeta Bhatia, SM ’93, PhD ’97, and colleagues could offer new hope to the thousands of Americans with chronic liver disease who are waiting for an organ...

item.title: Plants appear to detect the patter of falling rain
item.description: MIT engineers have found the first direct evidence that plant seeds can sense sounds in nature: Rice submerged in shallow water germinated 30% to 40% more quickly when exposed to vibrations from...

item.title: Prime Day takes $240 off Roborock’s Saros 20, one of our favorite robovacs
item.description: The best robot vacuums are the ones you barely have to think about, and the Roborock Saros 20 fits that description well. It’s why it’s one of our favorite robovac / mop hybrids, and thanks to...

item.title: The Meta Quest 3S is on sale for $297 — which is basically its old price
item.description: The Meta Quest 3S VR headset with 128GB of storage is $296.79 (about $53 off) at Amazon. This is Meta’s entry-level VR headset, which launched back in 2024 for $299.99 before getting a price...

item.title: The best Prime Day deals we found on our favorite gear
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day is on, and it’s happening for the next four days. Prime members can jump into the deals now until the sale officially ends at 3:01AM ET / 12:01AM PT June 27th. Many discounts...

item.title: Oracle’s 21,000 layoffs help drive its debt-fueled AI investments
item.description: Oracle is spending billions on data center infrastructure to support AI.
